eras.name: The Rococo Era
eras.description: A period of extreme opulence and artifice centered around the court of Versailles. Fashion was a tool for political status, characterized by voluminous silhouettes and intricate ornamentation.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A high-fashion historical portrait of a French noblewoman in 1750 wearing a silk Robe à la française with wide panniers, intricate lace engageantes, and pastel floral embroidery, set in a gilded Versailles salon, soft cinematic lighting.

eras.name: Empire Style
eras.description: Inspired by Neoclassicism and the Napoleonic campaigns, this era saw a radical shift toward simplicity and Greco-Roman silhouettes. Heavy corsetry was briefly abandoned in favor of high-waisted, lightweight gowns.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A Napoleonic era fashion illustration of a woman in a white muslin empire-waist gown with gold laurel embroidery, wearing a short velvet Spencer jacket, standing in a neoclassical marble hall.

eras.name: La Belle Époque
eras.description: The golden age of Parisian Haute Couture, led by Charles Frederick Worth. The S-bend silhouette and lavish use of lace and feathers defined the luxury of the pre-war upper class.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 1900s Parisian street scene featuring a woman in an elegant lace-trimmed afternoon gown with an S-curve silhouette, wearing an oversized feathered picture hat and carrying a silk parasol.

eras.name: The New Look & Golden Age
eras.description: Post-WWII recovery led by Christian Dior's 'New Look', reintroducing ultra-feminine silhouettes with cinched waists and full skirts. This era re-established Paris as the undisputed world fashion capital.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A black and white 1950s fashion photograph of a model in the Dior Bar Suit, featuring a structured cream silk jacket with a peplum and a voluminous pleated black wool skirt, posed by the Seine in Paris.

eras.name: Space Age & Youthquake
eras.description: A shift from haute couture to ready-to-wear (prêt-à-porter) led by designers like André Courrèges and Pierre Cardin. Fashion became geometric, futuristic, and youth-oriented.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 1960s French mod fashion featuring a model in a geometric white and silver vinyl mini dress, white patent leather go-go boots, and oversized round sunglasses, studio setting with pop-art colors.

eras.name: The YSL Revolution
eras.description: Yves Saint Laurent redefined femininity by introducing masculine tailoring into the female wardrobe, most notably the tuxedo for women, signaling a shift in gender norms and social power.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A dramatic 1970s fashion shot of a woman with slicked-back hair wearing a sharp black tuxedo suit with a white ruffled shirt, standing under a Parisian street lamp at night.

textiles.name: Dentelle d'Alençon
textiles.description: Known as the 'Queen of Lace', this needle lace is famous for its fine mesh and raised outlines.
textiles.technique: Hand-stitched needle lace using a horsehair support

textiles.name: Lyon Silk
textiles.description: High-quality jacquard and velvet silks that supplied the royal courts of Europe for centuries.
textiles.technique: Jacquard weaving with intricate floral and damask patterns

textiles.name: Breton Jersey
textiles.description: A durable, heavy-weight cotton knit originally used for naval uniforms, later popularized by Coco Chanel.
textiles.technique: Circular knitting with horizontal indigo stripes

textiles.name: Tweed de Maison Lesage
textiles.description: An avant-garde interpretation of tweed incorporating ribbons, sequins, and unconventional threads for Haute Couture.
textiles.technique: Hand-weaving on traditional looms with mixed-media yarns

climate.fashionImplication: The variable weather encourages sophisticated layering, trench coats, and high-quality knitwear, with a focus on 'trans-seasonal' pieces that transition from damp mornings to mild afternoons.

culturalInfluences: The Court of Louis XIV (The Sun King)
Existentialist movement of the Left Bank
The concept of 'L'art de vivre' (The art of living)
Colonial history and North African craftsmanship

emergingTrends: Digital fashion and NFTs in luxury houses
notableEvents: LVMH Prize for Young Fashion Designers
